era: Extreme storm season. Pacific Ocean at maximum low-frequency output. Microseisms + infrasound + ocean swell noise. The acoustic foundation of the Pacific Northwest coast.
ecosystem: Open Pacific Ocean, continental shelf, coastal Washington
family: Multi-mechanism physical acoustics (physical oceanography, seismology, atmospheric acoustics)
frequency_range: 0.05-200 Hz (sub-audible microseisms through audible surf roar)

theory_nodes.concept: Three simultaneous low-frequency sources: (1) Microseisms 0.05-0.5 Hz -- wave-wave interference generates seismic energy propagating through crust at 3-8 km/s, detectable continent-wide. (2) Infrasound 0.5-20 Hz -- breaking wave turbulence, surf zone interactions, atmospheric pressure fluctuations. Propagates thousands of km with minimal attenuation. (3) Ocean swell noise 20-200 Hz -- audible to all mammals, peak storm energy 50-100 Hz. SPL during storm: 120-130 dB re 1 µPa. All three simultaneous during a major Pacific storm event.
theory_nodes.description: The three-mechanism Full Pacific Infrasound event -- simultaneous from microseisms to swell noise.

theory_nodes.concept: Pacific Ocean scale: 165.25 million km², 30% of Earth's surface. Winter storm wave heights 6-15 meters; wave energy scales with the square of height. Long-period swell 14-22 seconds carries accumulated energy efficiently from 2,000-6,000 km offshore. During extreme events the PNSN records elevated microseismic activity continent-wide: the ocean is literally shaking the continent. No biological sound source generates comparable low-frequency acoustic energy.
theory_nodes.description: The Pacific Ocean as the largest acoustic source on Earth.

theory_nodes.concept: Ecological dimensions: Blue whale calls 10-40 Hz propagate ocean basin-scale via SOFAR channel. Fin whale vocalizations 17-35 Hz within infrasound range. Elephant seals (breeding Washington/Oregon coast) hear down to infrasound. During extreme Pacific storm events all cetaceans in the Pacific must communicate within the elevated ambient noise floor. Migration orientation hypothesis: Pacific infrasound as long-distance acoustic landmark for migrating species.
theory_nodes.description: Marine species operating within the Pacific infrasound acoustic environment.

theory_nodes.concept: Aberdeen acoustic formation: Grays Harbor, where the Melvins formed, is 20-30 miles from the open Pacific. The surf zone at Westport, Ocean Shores, Moclips is continuously active. The Pacific's low-frequency output infiltrates the entire Grays Harbor basin. The frequency overlap between Melvins guitar fundamentals (30-62 Hz during Bullhead/Lysol era) and the Pacific swell noise band is direct. The pairing with S36 345 (Melvins, E=9) is acoustically coherent: both operate at the lowest frequencies of their respective domains, from the same geographic origin.
theory_nodes.description: Aberdeen at the edge of the Pacific -- the acoustic connection to Melvins Peak Sludge.

theory_nodes.concept: E=9 calibration: E=10 is reserved for three S36 entries at the cultural ceiling. E=9 in the SPS track = events at the physical maximum for a given category. Full Pacific Infrasound during an extreme event is the maximum low-frequency acoustic event in the PNW ecosystem. The source area (165M km²) exceeds any biological sound source by orders of magnitude. Frequency penetration: infrasound passes through mountains, buildings, the ground. During a Pacific storm the low-frequency content permeates the entire Pacific Northwest -- from coast to Cascades, Vancouver Island to Columbia River.
theory_nodes.description: E=9 -- the SPS summit foundation, the largest sound source on Earth at extreme output.

cross_references.relationship: E=9 + E=9 = E=18. The summit foundation pairing. Shared frequency range (guitar 30-62 Hz overlapping infrasound upper range), shared Aberdeen geography, shared philosophy. The most acoustically coherent pairing in the summit.

SPS escalates from E=9 (ocean acoustic foundation) to E=10 (full atmospheric/hydrological mountain storm). Pacific infrasound is the acoustic bed; Cascades Full Storm is the mountain expression of the same energy system.
The previous SPS E=10. Full Pacific Infrasound is the low-frequency foundation that underlies all other SPS entries in the summit, including the prior apex.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=9 + E=9 = E=18. The summit opens. Shared frequency territory, shared geography, shared compositional philosophy of relentless maximum low-frequency output without concession.

milestones: ACOUSTIC FOUNDATION OF THE PACIFIC NORTHWEST
